title: Combination Study of RMC-4630 and Sotorasib for NSCLC Subjects With KRASG12C Mutation After Failure of Prior Standard Therapies

eligibility.text: Subject must be ≥18 years of age.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the trial can recruit participants within a specific age range, which is important for feasibility.

eligibility.text: Subject must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRASG12C NSCLC (not amenable to curative surgery) that has progressed on prior standard therapies (no more than 3 prior lines of therapies are allowed)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and likely to show a treatment effect.
This criterion serves as an exclusion criteria, not directly related to statistical power or feasibility.
This criterion protects patients from potential harm by excluding those with CNS tumors.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals at risk of complications from these conditions.
This criterion protects patients from potential harm by excluding those with pre-existing cardiac issues.

eligibility.text: Known impairment of GI function that would alter the absorption
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance and data quality, as it may impact treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within past 2 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential harm by excluding those with active autoimmune diseases that could interfere with treatment.

eligibility.text: History of severe allergic reactions to any of the study intervention components
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals with a history of severe allergic reactions to potential study interventions.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedures within 28 days or non-study-related minor procedures within 7 days of treatment.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety and data quality by excluding individuals who may experience complications from surgery or other medical interventions.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with KRASG12C inhibitor and/or SHP2 inhibitor
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals who have received prior treatment with KRASG12C inhibitors or SHP2 inhibitors.

endpoints.text: Objective Response Rate (ORR) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Clinically Significant Changes in Vital Signs
Clinically Significant Changes in Laboratory Tests
Trough and Approximate Peak Concentrations of RMC-4630
Trough and Approximate Peak Concentrations of Sotorasib
Duration of Response (DOR) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Disease Control Rate (DCR) as Assessed Per RECIST v.1.1
Progression-free Survival (PFS) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Incidence, Nature and Severity of TEAEs, SAEs

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)
